subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Position vector r→COM=m1r→1+m2r→2+m3r→3 m1+m2+m3

r→COM =2M×0+2M×4i^+2M×4j^6M

⇒r→=43i^+43j^

|r→|=432+432=423

Therefore, x=3.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Let vp be the velocity of ball P just before collision. Therefore, applying conservation of energy for P (between the moment it is released and the moment just before collision),

mgl+0=12mvp2

⇒vp=2gl

          =2×10×0.2

         =2 m s-1 

Since both balls have equal masses and the collision is perfectly elastic, the velocities of both the balls will get interchanged.

Therefore, the velocity of ball Q just after the collision is 2 m s-1.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
solution:
Statement I: Total kinetic energy of a system equals $\sum_i \frac{1}{2}m_i v_i^2$. This is TRUE by definition.
Statement II: Total KE can be decomposed as $KE_{total} = \frac{1}{2}M v_{cm}^2 + \sum_i \frac{1}{2}m_i v_i'^2$, where the first term is kinetic energy of center of mass motion and the second is kinetic energy of particles relative to the center of mass. This is TRUE by the energy decomposition theorem (Koenig's theorem).
Both statements are true.

Let us suppose there is an element of length dx at a distance x from left must end.

Mass of this element is dm=ρdx=ρ01-x2L2

The centre of mass of this element is Xcm=∫xdm∫dm

=ρ0∫0lx-x2L2xdx∫0lρ01-x2L2dx

=L22-L44L2L-L33L2=L242L3=3L8

Hence, the value of α=8.

solution: $\text { mass of disc }=\mathrm{m}$ $\text { mass of cut part }=\frac{m}{16}$ $X_{c o m}=\frac{m \times 0-\frac{m}{16} \times 15}{m-\frac{m}{16}}$ $=1 \mathrm{~cm} .$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Apply principle of conservation of momentum along $x$-direction, $$ \begin{aligned} m u &=m v_1 \cos 45^{\circ}+M v_2 \cos 45^{\circ} \\ m u &=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\left(m v_1+M v_2\right) \end{aligned} $$ Along $y$-direction, $$ \begin{aligned} &o=m v_1 \sin 45^{\circ}-M v_2 \sin 45^{\circ} \\ &o=\left(m v_1-M v_2\right) \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \end{aligned} $$
Coefficient of restution $e=1$ $$ =\frac{v_2-v_1 \cos 90}{u \cos 45} $$ ( $\because$ Collision is elastic) $$ \begin{aligned} &\Rightarrow \frac{v_2}{\frac{u}{\sqrt{2}}}=1 \\ &\Rightarrow u=\sqrt{2} v_2 \end{aligned} $$ Solving eqs (i), (ii), \& (iii), we get mass of unknown particle, $M=m$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & d m=\sigma d A \\ & \begin{aligned} d & =\sigma(d x)(d y)=\frac{\sigma_0 x}{a b}(d x)(d y) \\ x_{c o m} & =\frac{\int x d m}{\int d m}=\frac{\int x \frac{\left(\sigma_0 x\right)}{a b}(d x)(d y)}{\int_0 \frac{\sigma_0}{a b}(d x)(d y)} \\ & =\frac{\int_0^a x^2 d x \int_0^b d y}{\int_0^b x d x \int_0^b d y}=\frac{2 a}{3} \\ y_{c o m} & =\frac{\int y d m}{\int d m}=\frac{\int y\left(\frac{\sigma_0 x}{a b}\right)(d x)(d y)}{\int \frac{\sigma_0 x}{a b}(d x)(d y)}\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$
$=\frac{\int_0^a x d x \int_0^b y d y}{\int_0^a x d x \int_0^b d y}=\frac{b}{2}$
i.e., $\vec{r}_{\mathrm{com}} \equiv\left(\frac{2 a}{3}, \frac{b}{2}\right)$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
As we know, impulse is the area under the curve of the force vs. time graph. So, analysing each figure area under curve.

a A1=12×1.0×0.5=14 N‐s

b A2=0.5×2=1 N‐s

c A3=12×1×0.75=38 N‐s

d A4=12×2×0.5=12 N‐s

From above calculations, area under curve in figure (b) is maximum. Thus, fig (b) impulse is highest.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
options: Impulse and average force acting on the object will be same for both the cases.
Impulse will be same for both the cases but the average force will be different.
Average force will be same for both the cases but the impulse will be different.
Average force and impulse will be different for both the cases.
solution:
Impulse = Change in momentum

I=ΔP

Therefore, in both the cases the impulse is same.

Average force is defined as,

Favg =ΔPΔt

Since the time in the two cases is different, the average force will be different.

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
Given, M=200 MeV c-2 and m=1 GeV c-2, now use the concept of conservation of linear momentum, Mv0=mv⇒v=Mv0m  ...1.

Now use the conservation of energy, 

12Mv02=12mv2+∆E  ..2, here ∆E=13.6 eV, for the hydrogen atom in the ground state, now from equation, 1 & 2, 12Mv02=143∆E×108 eV=N4,

⇒N4=514 eV⇒N=51.

$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}^2=\frac{1}{2}(10)(3)^2-\left[\frac{1}{2}(15)(2)^2\right]$
$\Rightarrow 90-60=30=3000 x^2$

subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
solution:
The kinetic energy and the momentum of a moving object are related by

K=p22m   ...1

Hence, the ratio of the kinetic energies of two objects having masses m and m' can be calculated as follows:

KK'=p22mp22m'=m'm   ...2

Substitute the ratio of the kinetic energies into equation (2) and simplify to obtain the ratio of their masses.

⇒169=m'm⇒m:m'=9:16

Applying momentum conservation, (just before and just after collision)

mv=M+mv1

v=M+mmv1=610×10-3×2×9.8×9.8×10-2

≈831.55 m s-1
